data.content: Now I can see the anti-patterns. Let me modernize the tests:
data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the test file and the issues. Let me modernize it with MSTest best practices:

1. Make the class `sealed` 
2. Remove nullable `TestContext` - make it non-nullable (or remove if not used)
3. Replace `[TestInitialize]` with constructor for simple sync setup
4. Fix test naming to be descriptive
5. Fix swapped Assert.AreEqual arguments (expected, actual - first one had them reversed)
6. Replace `[ExpectedException]` with `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>`
7. Replace hard cast + Assert.IsTrue with proper assertions


8. Replace the manual data-driven loop with `[DataRow]` attribute

Since MSTest 3.6.3 is being used, `Assert.ThrowsExactly` is definitely available—it was introduced in v3.2+. The packages are already on the latest version, so I can use all the modern features. Now I'll write the modernized test file with a sealed class, constructor-based setup, and descriptive test names. I should verify that `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` is actually available in MSTest 3.6.3 before using it, since I'm not entirely certain about the exact version it was introduced. For the data-driven tests, I'll apply `[DataRow]` attributes with `[TestMethod]` to parameterize the test cases.

data.content: Two issues: missing implicit usings and MSTest 3.6.3 doesn't have `Assert.ThrowsExactly` (added in 3.7+). Let me fix both:

arguments.old_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.6.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
arguments.new_str:
<PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.7.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.7.3" />
  </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
Two issues:
1. The `PriceCalculator.cs` is missing `using System;` (needed for `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` in .NET 8 with implicit usings disabled)
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` might not be available in MSTest 3.6.3

Let me check the project - it targets net8.0 but might not have implicit usings enabled. Let me check if `ImplicitUsings` is set. Looking at the csproj, there's no `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>` so implicit usings are NOT enabled.


Actually, I'm second-guessing myself on the defaults. In .NET 6+, `ImplicitUsings` typically defaults to enabled with the standard SDK, but this project explicitly doesn't have it set, which means it's disabled here.

The real problem is that `Assert.ThrowsExactly` isn't available in MSTest 3.6.3 — that method was added in a later version. I should switch to `Assert.ThrowsException<T>` instead, which is the standard MSTest v3 approach, or bump the package version up. I need to update the MSTest package to 3.7+ and also handle the implicit usings issue with the preview SDK by either explicitly enabling it in the project file or adding the necessary using directives.

    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.6.3" />

data.reasoningText:
`Assert.ThrowsExactly` is still not found in 3.7.3. Let me check what version it was actually introduced in. Maybe it's MSTest v4.x only.

Let me check the MSTest docs/changelog. Actually, `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` was introduced in MSTest 3.8.0 I think. Or maybe it's an MSTest v4 feature. Let me try a higher version or check what's available.

Actually, looking at this more carefully - `Assert.ThrowsExactly` might have been introduced in MSTest 3.8.0 or even 4.0.0. 

I should look at what versions are actually available to see when this method was added.

data.reasoningText:
`Assert.ThrowsExactly` is an MSTest v4 feature. The latest is 4.2.2. Let me update to MSTest 4.x. In MSTest 4, you can use the consolidated `MSTest` package instead of separate TestAdapter/TestFramework packages. Let me update the project to use the modern approach.

Actually, for MSTest v4, the recommended approach is to use either:
1. `MSTest` meta-package (includes TestFramework + TestAdapter + Analyzers)
2. Or `MSTest.Sdk` as the project SDK

Let me use the `MSTest` meta-package approach since it's simpler to migrate to.

data.content:
All 5 tests pass. Here's a summary of the modernizations applied:

| Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|
| Non-sealed test class | `public sealed class` |
| Unused nullable `TestContext` | Removed |
| `[TestInitialize]` for sync setup | Field initializer (`new()`) |
| Generic `Test1` name | Descriptive `Method_Scenario_Expected` naming |
| Swapped `Assert.AreEqual` args | Fixed to `(expected, actual)` |
| `[ExpectedException]` attribute | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` |
| Hard cast + `Assert.IsTrue` | `Assert.IsInstanceOfType<T>()` + exact equality |
| Manual foreach data-driven loop | `[DataRow]` parameterized tests |

Also upgraded from MSTest 3.6.3 (separate packages) to MSTest 4.2.2 (unified `MSTest` package) which provides `Assert.ThrowsExactly` and the latest analyzers.
